steps.title: Use the one-moon criterion
steps.content: The statement needs two dwarf planets that, like Earth, have exactly one moon. The table identifies Eris and Makemake as the two planets that meet this condition.

steps.title: Check the planet pairs
steps.content: Choice B includes Haumea, which has two moons. Choice C includes Pluto and Haumea. Choice D includes Ceres, which has no moons. Only Eris and Makemake both have exactly one moon. The answer is A.
